title: Emergent Dynamic Magnetic Ground State in a Mixed 3d/5d Heavy Fermion System CaCu3Ir4O12
authors: J. Ming, Abhisek Bandyopadhyay, G. B. G. Stenning, M. T. F. Telling, N. N. Wang, G. Wang, J. -G. Cheng, D. T. Adroja
abstract: Quantum-disordered magnetic ground states are challenging to identify in three-dimensional (3D) oxides, where strong exchange pathways typically favour long-range magnetic order or spin freezing. The quadruple perovskite $\mathrm{CaCu_3Ir_4O_{12}}$, crystallizing in the cubic $Im\bar{3}$ structure, provides a 3D lattice where $\mathrm{Cu^{2+}}$ $3d$ moments are coupled to an extended Ir $5d$ network, offering a rare platform for probing quantum-disordered magnetism in a mixed $3d/5d$ electron system. Here, we combine bulk probes, including DC and AC magnetic susceptibility, and heat capacity measurements (down to $50~\mathrm{mK}$), along with the local microscopic probe muon spin relaxation ($μ$SR) (down to $40~\mathrm{mK}$), to investigate the true magnetic ground state of $\mathrm{CaCu_3Ir_4O_{12}}$. Despite strong antiferromagnetic interactions ($θ_{\mathrm{W}} \sim -200~\mathrm{K}$, with an applied-field dependence), no signature of long-range magnetic ordering or spin freezing is detected down to the lowest measured temperatures. Furthermore, our in-depth zero-field (ZF) and longitudinal-field (LF) $μ$SR characterizations confirm strong quantum spin fluctuations and the truly dynamic nature of the local moments down to $40~\mathrm{mK}$. These results establish $\mathrm{CaCu_3Ir_4O_{12}}$ as a promising 3D quantum-disordered magnet and a well-characterized platform for exploring fluctuation-dominated states in correlated $3d/5d$ oxides.
summary: This paper identifies CaCu3Ir4O12 as a rare 3D quantum-disordered magnet with heavy-fermion characteristics. By using muon spin relaxation, the authors demonstrate that the system maintains dynamic spin fluctuations down to 40 mK, ruling out conventional magnetic ordering or spin-glass freezing.
main_problem: The study aims to determine whether the 3D oxide CaCu3Ir4O12 possesses a genuine quantum-disordered magnetic ground state, such as a quantum spin liquid, or if it undergoes long-range magnetic ordering or spin freezing.
main_result: The researchers found no evidence of long-range magnetic ordering or spin freezing down to 40 mK, instead observing persistent, truly dynamic quantum spin fluctuations and heavy-fermion behavior.
method: The study combines bulk thermodynamic probes (DC/AC magnetic susceptibility, heat capacity) with local microscopic probes (zero-field and longitudinal-field muon spin relaxation, muSR).
model_or_system: The system is a chemically ordered 3D quadruple perovskite, CaCu3Ir4O12, featuring a mixed 3d/5d electron configuration where Cu2+ (3d) moments are coupled to an extended Ir 5d network.
key_observables: Magnetic susceptibility (chi), heat capacity (Cp), muon spin relaxation rate (lambda), stretched exponent (beta), and Weiss temperature (theta_W).
important_parameters: Temperature regime down to 40 mK; Weiss temperature theta_W ~ -200 K; large Sommerfeld coefficient gamma ~ 500 mJ/mol/K^2; scaling exponent gamma ~ 0.74.
main_assumptions: The sample is assumed to be nearly pure (97% phase purity) and the minor impurity phases (IrO2 and Ir-metal) are assumed to be weakly paramagnetic and not affecting the bulk ground state.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows Rietveld-fitted PXRD patterns and the structural coordination of Cu-O bonds; other figures include log-log plots of magnetic properties and muon spin asymmetry curves.
paper_structure: The paper begins with structural characterization via XRD, moves to bulk magnetic and thermodynamic measurements (susceptibility, magnetization, heat capacity), and concludes with local-probe muSR analysis to confirm the dynamic nature of the ground state.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Fig 1. (a) Rietveld-refined powder X-ray diffraction pattern of CaCu3Ir4O12 at room temperature. The observed and calculated data are, respectively, represented by open black circles and red solid line, the difference between observed and calculated patterns by solid blue line. The top, middle, and bottom vertical green ticks, respectively, indicate the Bragg positions of CaCu3Ir4O12, IrO2, and Ir-metal phase. (b) Crystal structure of CaCu3Ir4O12. (c) Cu planes with Ir atoms, and (d) corner-shared IrO6 octahedral units.

Fig 2. (a) Temperature dependence of the DC magnetic susceptibility, 𝜒(T), measured at 50 kOe under zero-field-cooled (ZFC) and field-cooled (FC) conditions. The solid black line represents the Curie–Weiss fit. Inset: Respective inverse susceptibility ([χ – χ0]-1) versus temperature plot together with linear CW fitting.(b) Low-temperature FC susceptibility measured under applied magnetic fields from 0.5 to 50 kOe. Open symbols represent experimental data, while solid lines denote power-law fits performed over field-dependent temperature ranges selected for each dataset. where the fitted regions exhibit approximately linear behaviour. (c) Field-dependent isothermal magnetization M(H) curves...

Fig 3. (a) Temperature dependence of real part of the AC magnetic susceptibility data measured between 2 and 300 K, and (b) 0.05 and 4 K with an excitation field of 𝐻ac = 3 Oe in selected frequencies.

Fig 4. (a) Temperature dependence of total specific heat Cp measured from 2–150 K in zero and applied different magnetic fields, Inset: Respective T-divided Cp data plots against temperature. (b) Magnetic specific heat (Cmag) in the 0.05 – 4 K range under zero and applied fields. Solid coloured lines represent respective theoretical fits using Eqn. 2. (c) Magnetic entropy (Sm) in the 0.05 – 4 K range in both zero and applied fields. (d) Field dependence of γ (left y-axis) and α (right y-axis), (e) Field- square dependence of nuclear Schottky term (δ), and (f) Field dependence of the coefficient D associated with the 𝑇(−𝑙𝑜𝑔𝑇).
